name: Paper Chaser MCP
description_for_model: Use Paper Chaser MCP as a trust-graded evidence tool. Default path: research for discovery, literature review, known-item recovery, citation repair, and regulatory routing; follow_up_research for one grounded question over a saved searchSessionId; resolve_reference for citations, DOI strings, arXiv IDs, URLs, and regulatory references; inspect_source to inspect one sourceId from research; get_runtime_status when behavior differs across environments. If the server is intentionally running with PAPER_CHASER_TOOL_PROFILE=expert, you may also use raw and provider-specific tools such as search_papers_smart, search_federal_register, get_cfr_text, and explicit ScholarAPI/OpenAlex paths.
conversation_starters: Research retrieval-augmented generation for coding agents and show only the most trustworthy findings.
Resolve this incomplete reference and tell me the safest next step plus any alternatives.
Research the regulatory history of California condor under 50 CFR 17.95 and call out any evidence gaps.
Use the searchSessionId from my last research run to answer one grounded follow-up question.
Inspect one source from my last research run and tell me whether it is safe to rely on.
Find the Federal Register notice behind this ECOS species review and then show me the affected CFR text.
Find papers on graphene with accessible full text, then fetch the best ScholarAPI text or PDF.
notes: This sample points at mcp-tools.full.json and assumes PAPER_CHASER_TOOL_PROFILE=expert when you want the raw and provider-specific expert surface to be advertised.
If you want the safest low-context default, use mcp-tools.core.json with PAPER_CHASER_TOOL_PROFILE=guided.
Resources are surfaced from tool outputs via resourceUris and searchSessionId handles.
If research abstains or asks for clarification, treat that as a real result rather than filling in unsupported synthesis.
For broken references, start with resolve_reference; for clearly regulatory references, use research first or pivot into the Federal Register/CFR expert tools when needed.
The server remains universal; these assets are packaging guidance, not a separate build.
